ruleset.name: Multithreading
ruleset.description: Rules that flag issues when dealing with multiple threads of execution.

rule.name: DontCallThreadRun
rule.message: Don't call Thread.run() explicitly; call Thread.start() to execute in a new thread. Consider using coroutines instead.
rule.description:
Calling thread.run() executes the run() method in the current thread, not a new one. Use thread.start() to actually create a new thread. For Kotlin prefer coroutines (launch {}) over raw threads.
Kotlin equivalent of Java rule: category/java/multithreading.xml/DontCallThreadRun

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.

rule.name: UseConcurrentHashMap
rule.message: Use ConcurrentHashMap instead of HashMap for concurrent access. Consider using a coroutine-safe data structure.
rule.description:
HashMap is not thread-safe. In multi-threaded code, concurrent reads/writes without synchronization can corrupt the map. Use ConcurrentHashMap for safe concurrent access, or consider Kotlin coroutine-friendly alternatives.
Kotlin equivalent of Java rule: category/java/multithreading.xml/UseConcurrentHashMap

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.

rule.name: UseNotifyAllInsteadOfNotify
rule.message: Call notifyAll() rather than notify() to wake all waiting threads. Consider using coroutines instead.
rule.description:
notify() wakes only one thread waiting on an object's monitor, chosen arbitrarily. notifyAll() wakes all waiting threads and avoids missed wake-up bugs. For Kotlin prefer coroutine primitives (Mutex, Channel, StateFlow) over raw synchronization.
Kotlin equivalent of Java rule: category/java/multithreading.xml/UseNotifyAllInsteadOfNotify

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.
